title: OEBF Profile
description: Cross-section profile for sweep geometry. SVG defines the shape in absolute metre coordinates; assembly defines layer composition and thicknesses in metres.

svg_file.description: Filename of the SVG file for this profile, relative to the profiles/ directory. SVG coordinates are absolute metres — see OEBF-GUIDE for conventions.

width.description: Total profile width in metres. Must equal the sum of all assembly layer thicknesses.

height.description: Profile height in metres. Null means height is taken from path geometry (for walls) or element height parameter (for columns, beams).

origin.description: 2D attachment point within the profile cross-section, in metres. This point is placed on the sweep path. Typically (0, 0) for left-face alignment or (width/2, 0) for centre alignment.

alignment.description: Which face of the profile is placed on the sweep path. left-face: the X=0 edge tracks the path. right-face: the X=width edge tracks the path. center: the profile midpoint tracks the path. top-face / bottom-face apply to horizontal profiles.

assembly.description: Ordered list of layers from face 0 outward. Layer boundaries are derived from cumulative thickness: Each layer occupies a contiguous X range: layer N starts at the sum of thicknesses for layers 1 through N−1 and ends at the sum through layer N. No SVG parsing is required to determine layer boundaries.

thickness.description: Layer thickness in metres. Tools display this as millimetres (e.g. 0.100 → 100mm).
